summary: Good‑quality soundboard capture of a mixed show highlighted by the rare electric “Monkey & the Engineer” and a solid second set with Dylan.
review: The recording is generally clear with well‑balanced vocals and Phil’s bass nicely isolated, making it one of the better SBD tapes from this era. Musically the night is uneven – the first set feels polite while the second set shines thanks to guest Bob Dylan, a rare full‑band electric “Monkey & the Engineer”, and strong moments like “How Long Blues” and “Not Fade Away”. Some reviewers note vocal flubs and a lack of tightness, but the rarity of the setlist keeps the show worthwhile.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the recording quality (good‑to‑excellent) and the second‑set highlights, while several point out performance slip‑ups; the weighted average yields a solid but not stellar rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and balanced; vocals and Phil’s bass are well isolated. No major dropouts reported, though some listeners describe it as merely “decent”.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is polite and a bit restrained; second set picks up energy with Dylan’s chaotic guest spot, the rare electric “Monkey & the Engineer”, and strong blues numbers. Overall flow is uneven but the latter half delivers the memorable moments.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar licks on “Not Fade Away” are praised, but several reviewers note lyric flubs and shaky vocal delivery throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Consistently highlighted for smooth, great bass lines and excellent isolation in the mix.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir receives mixed feedback – one reviewer calls him an "A‑hole of the Year" while others find his contributions acceptable.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s Hammond organ is described as sounding tired on a few tunes and being turned down to make room for guest guitarist Spencer Davis.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum/space segment is mentioned positively, but no specific criticism or standout praise is given.
